text.title: AI-Feature Incident Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the incident-record shape, the mitigation log, and the postmortem-draft sections.

section.title: Required incident-record fields

list.item: incident_id (string, deterministic ID)
opened_at_utc (ISO timestamp)
class (enum: hallucination_spike, jailbreak_observed, refusal_rate_anomaly, quality_regression, unknown)
class_confirmed_at_utc (ISO timestamp; MUST be set within 10 min of open if class != unknown)
sli_signals (object: latency_p99, refusal_rate, quality_score, hallucination_flag_rate, with deltas from 7-day baseline)
model_version (string, from registry)
prompt_version (commit SHA from `prompts/`)
mitigations (array of objects: kind, applied_at_utc, by_user, reversal_command, observed_effect)
resolved_at_utc (ISO timestamp or null)
severity (enum: SEV1, SEV2, SEV3)
postmortem_due_by_utc (5 business days after resolved_at_utc for SEV1/SEV2)

section.reference: Authoritative JSON Schema for the incident record.

list.item: Every mitigation appended in order with timestamp, command run, reversal command, and 5-min-post observation.
Mitigations that did not work MUST stay in the log with `observed_effect: no_change` or `worse` — never deleted.
Reversal command for every applied mitigation MUST be valid and tested in the staging registry before incident time.
which_class: which of the four AI-incident classes fired, with the SLI delta that triggered it.
which_signal_fired: which observability pillar (latency / cost / quality / drift) first surfaced the issue and at what timestamp.
which_root_cause_branch: model version drift, prompt drift, retrieval drift, eval-set staleness, upstream provider change, or other (named).
which_harden_step: the persistent fix that closes this class going forward (new eval, new filter, new alert, new test).

antipattern.description: Incident-record `class` stays `unknown` past 10 minutes without an escalation entry.
antipattern.reason: r1 violation. Forces accountability when class is unclear.
r2 violation. All mitigations must be reversible by single command.

antipattern.description: Postmortem auto-published without team review signature.
antipattern.reason: r5 / sibling-rule violation. Postmortem is reviewed before publish.

antipattern.description: quality_regression declared with no eval-set run referenced in the last 48h.
antipattern.reason: r3 violation. Eval-set freshness is required.

list.item: incident_id populated.
class confirmed within 10 min of open.
Every mitigation has a reversal_command.
postmortem_due_by_utc set for SEV1 / SEV2.
Postmortem draft includes the four required sections.
